{{Short description|Cluster of Corridor-type Kofun Tomb in Osaka, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Takaida Yokoanabos | native_name =高井田横穴墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Takaida Yokoana Group-2-3,4,5.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Takaida Yokoanabos,No. 3, 4, 5 of the 2nd branch | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Takaida Yokoanabos | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|34|34|22.3|N|135|38|16.6|E|region:JP-27|display=inline}}; {{Coord|34.572861|N|135.637944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara, Osaka]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.late 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park, Museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|34|22.3|N|135|38|16.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Takaida Yokoanabos'''|高井田横穴墓群|Takaida yokoana}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} cliff of located in the Takaida neighborhood of city of [[:en:Kashiwara, Osaka|Kashiwara, Osaka]], in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1922, with the area under protection expanded 1990.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216899 |title=高井田横穴墓群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Takaida Yokoanabos are located on the southernmost tip of the {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Ikoma Mountains|1=Mount Ikoma}}, on the slope of a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} hill facing the {{ill|Yamato River|en|Yamato River|WD=Q9001527|lt=Yamato River|1=Yamato River}} at an elevation of 30 to 50 meters.The site consists of more than 200 tombs each have an opening approximately one meter square, with a narrow entrance tunnel leading to a larger chamber within, up to nine square meters in area with a ceiling of 170 to 180 centimeters in height. About half of the tombs have a step cut to a width of about 10 centimeters at the boundary between the wall and the ceiling. This is of unknown purpose, but is thought to resemble the eaves of a house. There were uptown three buries in a single typically in wooden coffins whose presence and size can be determined by the position of nails found on the floor of the tombs. However, a large percentage of tombs had no nails, so it is uncertain if some burials were without coffins, or else coffins were made without the use of nails. The burials were also made with the head facing north, regardless of the orientation of the entry to the tomb. A few tombs have a built-in stone sarcophagus,which was presumably cloned out and reed for subsequent burials. The tombs are in clusters with two to four tombs forming a unit, with between 10 and 15 units in a cluster, presumably corresponding to familial and clan groupings. The tombs are noted for a profusion of line engravings, depicting people, ships, birds, horses, fish, trees, flowers, swirls, suns, houses, etc. Some of the drawings show details such as the clothes of the people and the gondola-like shape of the hull of a boat. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} including earrings, beads and other jewelry, horse harnesses and red-pigmented pottery has also been found. Construction began in the first half of the 6th century and seems to have continued until the 7th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The site was discovered in 1917, but since many tombs have been open for a long time, it has not been confirmed whether all of the drawings are contemporary with the original construction.
There is also considerable speculation as to who made this cluster of tombs, as this style is very rare for the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of Japan, and no settlement traces have been found in the near vicinity. The construction techniques and arrangement of bodies is similar to site sin the {{ill|Higo Province|en|Higo Province|WD=Q259705|lt=Higo region|1=Higo Province}} of [[Kyushu]], whereas the style of clothing depicted in the drawings and depictions of boats point to a continental influence. On the other hand, votive ''{{ill|Kamado|en|Kamado|WD=Q12177411|lt=Kamado|1=Kamado}}'' rice cookers, a common offering in ''{{ill|Toraijin|en|Toraijin|WD=Q11561926|lt=Toraijin|1=Toraijin}}'' tombs of settlers from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] have been found in only two of the tombs, and similar contemporary cave tomb clusters on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] are not similar in style.
The site has is open to the public as the Takaida Yokoana Park, and the grave goods [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the tombs are displayed at the adjacent Kashiwara City History Museum. Then site is a short walk from [[:en:Takaida Station (Kashiwara)|Takaida Station]] on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
